untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of non-random price action patterns including: (1) multiple price tests with clustering detection—requires lookback window analysis not available in indicator-only format; (2) reversal candlestick pattern recognition—requires pattern matching logic beyond simple indicator comparison; (3) divergence on momentum indicators—requires comparing price extrema to indicator extrema across time, not expressible as single-bar conditions; (4) price deceleration measurement—requires rate-of-change analysis not available. Pivot levels (s1/r1) used as proxy for support/resistance, but core edge-validation logic cannot be translated to structured conditions.
translation_notes: GRIMES_DRY_4_1 emphasizes non-random price action at support/resistance as the core edge—specifically: clustering of tests, reversal patterns, divergences, and volume surges. These are pattern-recognition and multi-bar analysis requirements that exceed the scope of single-indicator threshold rules. The rule is fundamentally about detecting ORDER FLOW CONVICTION, not price levels alone. Recommend implementing as custom pattern detection module rather than threshold-based backtest rules. Favorable R:R ratio is mentioned but not quantified in source material. Entry uses pivot s1/r1 with volume confirmation as best available proxy; exit uses invalidation logic (close beyond level).
